observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal
Accountant to make necessary adjustments and record the identified valid
reconciling items as presented in the BRS. In addition, submit the unrecorded
disbursement vouchers to the Audit Team for audit.

We also recommended strict compliance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445,
Section 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and
Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the LCE:
a) Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to submit the remaining twenty
DVs together with its supporting documents to the MAO and strictly
adhere to the check disbursement process provided under Section 44 of
NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to record in the book of accounts the
unrecorded disbursements upon submission of the DVs and its
supporting documents by the Acting Municipal Treasurer, in order to
reflect the accurate balances of cash, equity and expense accounts of the
LGU in the financial statements and thereafter, submit the same to the
Audit Team for audit.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the:
a) GSO prepare the necessary SSMIs supported with RIS or
Acknowledgement Receipt for all supplies and materials already issued,
particularly those pertaining to previous years’ purchase and submit the
same to the Municipal Accountant; and
b) Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) as
supported by SSMIs and RIS submitted by the GSO to adjust the
affected Inventory accounts.

We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to rectify the erroneous
postings relative to Other Supplies and Materials for Distribution (1-04-02-990) and
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory (1-04-04-990).

We recommended that:
a) The Inventory Committee to consider the provisions of COA Circular No.
2020-006 re: Guidelines and Procedures in the Conduct of Physical
Count of PPE, Recognition of PPE Items Found at Station, and
Disposition for Non-existing/Missing PPE Items, for the One-Time
Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies;
b) The Accountant and General Services Officer complete the
reconciliation process to eliminate the discrepancies in the inventory
report and accounting records and effect the adjustments/ corrections;
c) The MAO and GSO maintain updated/ complete PPELCs and PC and
to regularly reconcile their records; and
d) The management construct a garage to secure the vehicles and
equipment from burglars and prevent them from direct exposure to
natural elements such as rain and sunlight that hasten their
deterioration.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct physical count of
Inventory for Supplies and Materials and prepare the RPCI for every semester of a
given year in accordance with NGAS Manual and submit the same to the Audit Team
and also to Accounting Office reconciliation records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately identify the
reconciling items of the of the affected reciprocal accounts and effect the adjustments
thereof in the respective books to present fairly the correct balances of said accounts
in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE require the MAO to update the subsidiary
records/ ledgers of Due to NGAs account and submit the same to the Audit Team as
soon as possible.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Review and reconcile the withheld taxes and the remittances to be made
to ensure that the correct amount of taxes are withheld and remitted to
the BIR;
b) Review and analyze thoroughly the journal entries made before posting
the same in the GL and SLs to ensure accurate recording of transactions
and reliable balances of accounts; and
c) Work back and trace the source documents to determine and identify
the unreconciled balances of the “Due to BIR” account and immediately
cause the remittance thereof to the BIR once identified/ reconciled.

We recommended that the:
a) GSO designate and MAO strictly adhere to Section 114, 119 and 120 of
the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I on the preparation of stock
cards and SLC with stock number on each type of supplies as
prescribed in the above manual; and
b) MAO adopt the perpetual inventory method as required in Section 114
of the NGAS Manual for LGUS, Volume I.

We recommended that the:
a) Municipal Accountant make full and appropriate disclosure of the
Road Network account in the Notes to Financial Statements as
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 and IPSAS; and
b) GSO Designate and the Inventory Committee conduct an inventory of
local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction and submit the required
Reports in accordance with the format provided under Annexes A and
C of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 to this
Office. Likewise, conduct the reconciliation of the list of inventory per
local road inventory report with those recorded in the Road Networks
account.

We recommended that Management:
a) Pursue the attainment of 100 per cent implementation of 20%
Development Fund, to maximize the desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes of the LGU as envisioned in
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-1;
b) Require the MPDC and Municipal Budget Officer to set definite and
realistic timelines of PPAs’ implementation in the proposed AIP; and
c) Require the Municipal Development Council to propose feasible priority
projects that can be implemented within the year, ensuring timely
delivery of benefits to the target beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LCE require the HRMO to:
a) Provide the detailed order indicating the duties and functions of the
hired JOWs;
b) Compel the requesting offices to provide detailed descriptions of the
work to be performed by the JOWs they intend to hire as these
descriptions will serve as the basis for the HRMO to prepare a
detailed order outlining the duties and functions of the hired JOWs.
c) Provide the duties and responsibilities for the highly technical JOWs
and a written justification outlining the necessity of their engagement;
and
d) Compel the JOWs to provide the duly accomplished and subscribed
PDS.

We recommended that the LCE direct the HRMO to ensure that deduction
due to tardiness are properly applied, photocopies of logbook, pass slips and detailed
accomplishment reports are attached to the DTRs of the hired JOWs.

We recommended that LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Resolve the difference of ₱6,737,331.98 and ₱3,576,291.80 in the financial
statements and Statement of Fund Utilization Report and prepare the
necessary journal entries to reconcile the balances in the books of
accounts, if warranted; and
b) Ensure that the current year’s appropriation balance is properly
presented in the Statement of Fund Utilization at year-end as well as in
the detailed breakdown in the Notes to FS.

We recommended that the:
a) Municipal DRRM Council prioritize the procurement of DRRM
equipment, and other equipage and only include PPAs in the LDRRMP
that will directly contribute to Disaster Prevention and Mitigation,
Disaster Preparedness, Disaster Response, and Disaster Rehabilitation
and Recovery;
b) Municipal Budget Officer refrain from charging expenditures to the
LDRRM Fund that are administrative in nature; and
c) Municipal Accountant exclude ineligible DRRM expenditures from the
monthly LDRRM utilization reports.

Additionally, we recommended revising the LDRRMP for CY 2025, if it is
found to be non-compliant with the above provisions.

We recommended that the Management instruct the MSWDO to adhere to
COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 on the submission of GAD
Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team in order to facilitate timely evaluation of
GAD PPAs and prompt communication of lapses, if any.

We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees:
a) Discontinue the payment of registration fees to various leagues and
associations for the conduct of regular or routine meetings and assemblies;
b) Consider proposing to their respective leagues that the conduct of 1-day
meetings may be done through teleconferencing to eliminate the incurrence
of traveling expenses and registration fees, or holding them at government-
operated function halls, session halls and event centers to minimize costs;
and
c) Exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid
incurrence of unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended that the LSB submit the quarterly reports (Report of SEF
Utilization) to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, and furnish copies of
the same to the Sanggunian Bayan and the Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG
within the prescribed periods.

Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and
submit the annual SEF utilization report to the LSB, which should then submit it to
the concerned offices and agencies.

We recommended that Management limit the payroll deductions to the order
of preference provided in the GAA and ensure that the approval of loan applications
of personnel should not cause their net take home pay to be below ₱5,000.00 as
required by the GAA.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer enforce strictly the
preparation of MRAAF of all Collecting Officers and prepare the CRAAF as
prescribed in Section 86 of the LTO Manual Book II.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the concerned Accounting
and Treasury personnel exert extra effort in preparing and submitting on time the
above-mentioned accounts including related supporting documents. We suggest that
such activities be properly monitored so that reports are submitted within the
reglementary period. Also, we enjoin Management to consider the timely submission
of reports as among the primary measures of performance of its employees.

However, to ensure full compliance, we recommended that the LCE:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Officer to
make reconciliation of previous years’ records with the GSIS;
b) MA and Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit all premium
contributions from prior years after reconciliation of records is made; and
c) Accounting Department to reconcile individual SL balances with the
reported balance of the account in the General Ledger and make
necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We commended the Management’s substantial compliance with laws, rules,
and regulations on the proper deduction and remittance of HDMF contributions and
loan repayments, as mandated under RA No. 9679.

However, to ensure full compliance, we recommended that the LCE:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Management
Officer to reconcile the balance of the Due to Pag-IBIG account from
prior years and CY 2024, and coordinate with Pag-IBIG if necessary to
ensure the correctness of the unremitted balance at year-end.
Furthermore, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to promptly remit all
outstanding premium contributions upon completion of the
reconciliation; and
b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Management
Officer to establish or develop an internal control system/procedure to
prevent delays in remittance and eliminate the occurrence of
unreconciled balances and/or unremitted premium contributions and
loan repayments.

We commended the Management’s substantial compliance with laws, rules,
and regulations on the proper deduction and remittance of PHIC premium
contributions, as mandated under RA No. 7875.

However, to ensure full compliance, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Management
Officer to reconcile the balance of the Due to PhilHealth account from
prior years and CY 2024, and coordinate with PhilHealth if necessary to
ensure the correctness of the unremitted balance at year-end.
Furthermore, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to promptly remit all
outstanding premium contributions upon completion of the
reconciliation; and
b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Management
Officer to establish or develop an internal control system/procedure to
prevent delays in remittance and eliminate the occurrence of
unreconciled balances and/or unremitted premium contributions.

We also recommended strict compliance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445, Sections 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We commended the Management, particularly the Office of the Municipal Accountant, the Property Supply Officer and the Inventory Team for their commitment and efforts to comply with the conduct of annual physical inventory of all its properties. Also, in adhering to Section 53b of GAAM, Volume III, by preparing and submitting Inventory Report on time and reconciled the same with the accounting records.

We recommended that Management require the:

MHO to render a report to the Municipal Accountant on the receipt, issuance, and balances of vaccines directly received from outside sources; and 
Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MHO, to determine the full cost of ending inventory of vaccines as at year end, if any and henceforth recognize and record future receipts and issuances of the same.

We recommended that Management facilitate the drafting and approval of the guidelines to be adopted in hiring of JO workers to strengthen the internal control pertaining thereto. Also, such guidelines shall cover all LGU JO applicants, both from executive and legislative departments.  

We recommended further that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Human Resource Department to oversee the hiring of JOs both for executive and legislative department to determine the appropriate personnel complement of the concerned offices. Also, the Accomplishment Reports and DTRs should be monitored and approved by the HRMO for evaluation and proper control.

We recommended that a resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan be passed to authorize the LCE to employ such Job Order employees in compliance with Section 77 of RA 7160. 

We further recommended that the Management prioritize the programs, projects, and activities to be done. Only those Offices that actually need job order workers shall hire assistants in order to avoid unnecessary government expenses. The LCE shall monitor and control strictly the hiring of Job Order workers performing utility, clerical and administrative functions based on necessity.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remind the LDRRMO to ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year and to optimize utilization of the fund to mitigate disaster risk and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MTO to deposit daily or the next banking day the collections/remittances, require the RCOs to remit collections regularly and deposit the remaining amount of 262.63 to the LBP. We also recommend that the LCE direct the MTO to submit on time to the Accounting Department the Report of Collections and Deposits for immediate and proper recording.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MT to provide additional column headings and include other required information to the permanent record book for the unissued and succeeding purchases of AFs for proper monitoring, control and complete record keeping in conformance with section 24 of COA Circular 92-382 and section 86 (D.1 and D.3) of the LTO Manual.

We recommended that the Management direct the BAC secretariat to religiously prepare the Annual Procurement Plan based on the approved annual budget of the agency. Require all department heads to submit their respective PPMPs within the deadline set and consolidate the same in the Annual Procurement Program of the agency for the year.

We commended the Management for the timely submission of the financial reports for CY 2022 on or before the reglementary periods in compliance with the above-cited regulations. Nonetheless, we recommended that the Management, thru its Office of the Municipal Accountant, continue in their commitment in achieving accuracy, reliability and transparency in financial reporting.

In view of the above post audit observations, we acknowledged Management in its effort to adhere to the rules and regulations on the grant of COVID-19 AHDP. However, we still recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials and employees to submit the required documents and the person in-charge to ensure completeness of documentary requirements before processing of transactions.

We recommended that the Management instruct the:

a. Municipal Treasurer to submit Monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF), supported with the individual Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) of the collecting and disbursing officers, to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month.

b.  Person in-charge to include in the CRAAF all kinds of applicable accountable forms mentioned above, especially checks and cash tickets. 

c. MT to prepare properly the CRAAF by checking the mathematical computations on additions and deductions, total footings, reconciliation of balances and double checking of the Serial Numbers of the Accountable Forms to assure the correctness and accuracy of the report submitted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person or the concerned employee to prepare and submit the attachments as required under Section 4.1.C.8.5 of the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular No, 2013-1 and provide sufficient details of the activities undertaken to facilitate validation of GAD programs and projects reported.

We recommended that the LCE require the MENRO to submit immediately the revised closure plan to EMB-DENR and eventually secure the ATC of the existing dumpsite. 

We likewise recommended that the Municipality cause the immediate closure of the LGU’s existing controlled dumpsite.  Such immediate action is necessary to address if not prevent, detrimental ecological issues and their effects on human health and to the environment.

the ICO-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Budget Officer to (i) comply with the provisions of IPSAS 24 on the preparation of Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts; and (ii) reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly so that any resulting discrepancies can be immediately verified and corrected;

the Local Finance Committee to ensure that budget for the ensuing years are prepared in accordance with the procedures, schedules and local budget preparation forms prescribed under the Budget Operation Manual for LGUs; and

the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and/or in the Notes to FS the (i) changes between the original and final budgets; (ii) material discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts; and (iii) reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements, to enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements.

the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of the individual TF bank
accounts and other assets with its corresponding liabilities to establish the correct
TF account balances in the books of accounts.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary
adjusting entry/ies after reconciliation to reflect the correct TF account balances in
the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to
expedite the titling process of the four identified parcels of land.

Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to
establish the breakdown of the difference between the recorded land account and
the identified parcels of land totaling P6,065,713.21.

In view of the foregoing audit observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (i) immediately deposit all collections with the authorized depository bank and ensure that all collections for the ensuing year are remitted and deposited promptly and intact; (ii) update the cashbook for Cash-Local Treasury daily and ensure proper reconciliation with the cash on hand and with the accounting records; and (iii) see to it that the Accountable Forms to be used for each fund are properly designated and ensure that it is properly reported in their respective funds; and
Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure the completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions; and (ii) adjust the balance of the accounts affected by the miscalculated collections and deposits to reflect a reliable amount in the financial statements.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the Municipal Accountant to (i) reconcile his FS and GL; and (ii) exert more effort in accomplishing the BRS and make sure that all reconciling items are traced and reflected in the books, if necessary;
require the Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to expedite the reconciliation of their respective records and henceforth comply with Sections 10 and 27 of the MNGAs for LGUs Vol. II in preparing the SL and Cashbooks; and
comply with DOF D.C. No. 01-2015 dated June 1, 2015 as amended by Sections 1 and 2 of DOF D.C. No. 01-2017 dated May 11, 2017 by closing the bank account with the PNB and transferring the same to the available AGDBs within the province.

With the foregoing, we reiterated the following recommendations from the previous year that the Municipal Mayor require:
the Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Budget Officer to (i) comply with the provisions of IPSAS 24 on the preparation of SCBAA and with the SCBAA template per Annex F of Item 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016; (ii) ensure the accuracy and consistency of balances in the reports; and (iii) reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly so that any resulting discrepancies can be immediately verified and corrected;
the Local Finance Committee to ensure that budgets for the ensuing years are prepared in accordance with the procedures, schedules, and local budget preparation forms prescribed under the Budget Operation Manual for LGUs; and
the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and in the Notes to FS on the (i) changes between the original and final budgets; (ii) material discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts; and (iii) reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements, to enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor along with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer strictly follow the provisions of DBM – DOF – DILG JMC No. 1 in planning the PPAs funded under the 20 percent DF and their implementation. See to it that the individual PAPs in the AIP is properly identified and refrain from inputting lump sum/generic term appropriation. Closely monitor the implementation of development projects and ensure that all infrastructure projects are implemented in accordance with its time frame to avoid delayed completion of the projects;
Municipal Engineer conduct applicable detailed engineering activities to serve as basis of the MPDC in properly evaluating the technical viability of proposed projects before inclusion in the list of priority projects to be included in the 20 percent DF in line with the requirement that development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready.  In this way, projects with issues, not feasible or has a remote possibility of being implemented may not be considered during the budget year; and
Municipal Accountant (i) be guided by  the requirements of Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 474 b(5) of R.A. No. 7160 for proper documentation of claims against government funds; (ii) submit the lacking documents mentioned above to establish the propriety of the disbursements; (iii) observe the proper recording of transactions and effect the necessary correcting entry for proper presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; and (iv) see to it that accurate amount of taxes are withheld from the suppliers pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 as amended under Revenue Regulation No. 11-2018.

The management has yet to institute appropriate action to designate ICO-Municipal Budget Officer who is more qualified for the position. It is to be emphasized that the currently designated ICO-Municipal Budget Officer is holding a first level position which is prohibited under Section 13(c), Rule IV of the 2017 Omnibus Rules on Appointments and Other Human Resource Actions (ORAOHRA) thus, preparation and submission of accurate reports was affected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive through the Human Resource Management Officer strictly observe the rules and regulations or requirements on Human Resource Action particularly on the designation procedure as provided in Section 13 (c), Rule IV of the 2017 Omnibus Rules on Appointments and Other Human Resource Actions (ORAOHRA).
observations.reason:
Personnel under first level position were still designated to perform the responsibilities of second level positions.

The management explained that the current organizational set up does not provide to designate personnel in the LGU to hold said position because there were no available second level personnel to be designated as such.  The other department heads are fully occupied of their duties and responsibilities.  All other personnel are either Administrative Assistant or Administrative Aides.  Under the present approved plantilla positions, the Department Heads, are without next in rank positions.  So, in this case, in order to continue the functions of the office, something must be done in the exigency of service.

They also commented that the position of the MPDC cannot be filled up yet because the former holder of the position has a case pending with the Ombudsman.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management discontinue the practice of procuring goods or services on reimbursement basis, except for petty  expenses. Strictly observe the provision of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in all government procurement.
observations.reason: Some disbursements were still made through reimbursement basis, however, the Audit Team did not issue Audit Observation Memorandum due to immaterial amount involved.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant  carefully analyze and review the accounting entries made on the accounts affected, reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect appropriate adjustments thereof to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the agency’s financial position.
observations.reason: Reciprocal accounts were still not reconciled as of CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant:

comply with the requirement of the perpetual inventory system wherein regular purchases shall be coursed thru the inventory account and issuances thereof shall be recorded as they take place except those purchased out of petty cash fund which shall be for immediate use and not for stock. Such case shall be charged immediately to the appropriate expense accounts. 

prepare JEVs to adjust/record issuances on the affected accounts and coordinate closely with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure reconciliation of their inventory records, also in respect of physical inventory taking.

regularly prepare and submit accurate and complete Summary of Supplies and Materials Issue (SSMI) with supporting Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) pursuant to Section 121 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, to the Accounting Unit for proper recording/posting of issuance in the books of accounts and Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs).
observations.reason: Purchases of supplies in CY 2020 were still recorded as outright expense.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
submit immediately to the Audit Team the required supporting documents
pertaining to the payment of expenditures totaling P12,741,250.51 to avoid possible
suspension and/or disallowance in audit. Henceforth, examine thoroughly the
vouchers and their supporting documents before processing and effecting
payments.
We also recommended to require the BAC Chairperson to submit to the Audit Team
for evaluation the written explanation/justification for not observing strict
compliance with the GPPB guidelines on mandatory posting of all emergency
procurements of the Municipality. Hereafter, comply with the existing rules and
regulations relative to government procurements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer
(MSWDO) to submit the valid IDs, photos taken during the actual receipt and the
specific documentary requirement for each beneficiary as enumerated under DSWD
Memorandum Circular No. 2020-09 or the certification from the Barangay that the
beneficiary is in need of assistance, as the case may be;
• Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims against government funds are duly
supported with proper and complete documentation; and
• Municipal Treasurer to return immediately the unutilized balance of P84,500.00 to
DSWD.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
GSO/Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical count of Municipal
properties; prepare the RPCPPE and reconcile the same with the accounting
records; and maintain Property Cards for each PPE category to ascertain the
existence and valuation of the recorded properties.
We also recommended to create an Inventory Committee for the purpose of one-
time cleansing of PPE account balances following the guidelines prescribed under
COA Circular No. 2020-06.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the (a) list of taxpayers showing
the amount due and collectible for the year to be used as basis in the setting-up of
the RPT and SET Receivables which should be based on the Assessment Roll of
the Municipal Assessor, and (b) list of delinquent taxpayers and the amount of
uncollected taxes as at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
determine the details of the Land account by tracing it from the available prior years’
records and inquiry from other offices, disclose in the FS the significant information
relevant to the properties and record the cost of the leased property under the
Investment Property, Land account.
We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Assessor in coordination with the
OIC-Municipal Treasurer process the titling of the four parcels of land.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the filing of claim against the
estate of the deceased former municipal official, through his legal heirs, for the
refund of the unliquidated CAs amounting to P156,240.00.
We also recommended to instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort in
completing the supporting documents necessary for the filing of the request for
authority to write-off of dormant accounts of P54,500.00 and reclassify the
Advances to Officers and Employees account to Other Receivables account for fair
presentation of the Receivables in the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
present the MOOE attributed to economic enterprises in the FS for PUEE for fair
reporting; and the Officer-in-Charge-PUEE to continue the evaluation of the
existing market fees and formulate and implement strategies to improve the
financial performance of the PUEE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
transfer the unutilized balance of funds held in trust by the Municipality to the GF
totaling P321,429.05 and make a request to the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an
Appropriation Ordinance for the use of the unutilized funds for COVID-19 related PPAs
in accordance with the aforementioned rules.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer (GSO) to complete the conduct of physical count of municipal properties; prepare the RPCPPE and reconcile the same with the accounting records; and maintain Property Cards for each PPE category to ascertain the existence and valuation of the recorded properties.
observations.managementAction: The GSO has already started the physical count of properties.
observations.reason: Due to various acquisitions of properties during the year as response to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• demand the immediate liquidation of the CAs from the concerned officials and employees and cause the withholding of their salaries for failure to do so or deduct the amounts from the money due them;
• refrain from granting additional cash advance equivalent to the amount of unreported disbursements; and
• prepare the supporting documents necessary for the filing of the request for authority to write-off of dormant accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already demanded for the liquidation of the CAs from concerned officials and employees. The Municipal Accountant is still completing the necessary documents for the filing of the request for authority to write-off dormant accounts and the necessary submission to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: There were still unliquidated CAs at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor file with the COA the request for authority to write-off the dormant receivables together with the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution and necessary supporting documents in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has already filed the request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts with the COA together with the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution and necessary supporting documents in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections promptly and intact in compliance with the afore-cited regulation.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Treasurer deposited all collections promptly and intact in compliance with the afore-cited regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Administrator to formulate strategies and implement measures to improve the financial performance of the PUEE.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-PUEE committed to expedite the revision of the different sector code rates to improve collection.
observations.reason: Due to outdated market fees and uncollected delinquent accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to:
• submit to the Audit Team at the beginning of the year, the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year and the monthly monitoring report; and
• inform the Auditors within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted, for validation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has submitted the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented at the beginning of the year and the monthly monitoring report; and the awarded projects were reported through the monthly report being submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The awarded projects were reported through the monthly report being submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to be guided by the DILG-DBM JMC in the formulation of their local development plans and AIP for 20 percent DF and exert more effort on its implementation to ensure timely execution and fully achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and the MPDC ensured that the local development plans and AIP were in accordance with the DILG-DBM JMC and that the projects were implemented on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF monthly and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to submit the same to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the report monthly to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and the SWM Board ensure the Municipality’s compliance with RA No. 9003 by expediting the preparation and revision of the 10-Year SWM Plan for submission to and approval of the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) to ensure that the programs and activities for SWM are consistent with the NSWMF which aims to protect the public health and the environment.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO-Designate started the preparation and revision of the 10-Year SWM Plan.
observations.reason: Due to lack of proper turnover of documents between the former MENRO and the current MENRO.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC, with the assistance of the Municipal GAD Focal Person to:
• institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation;
• undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the PPAs are responsive to the said issues; and
• include in the GPB the expenses related to the development of GAD database.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and the MGAD Focal Person commented that the proposed CBMS was not implemented due to pandemic restrictions as the activity employs a house-to-house data collection. Nevertheless, the Municipality has appropriated funds under Supplemental Budget No. 1 and Appropriation Ordinance No. 01-2021 dated February 24, 2021 for the development of GAD database amounting to P200,000.00 to officially set-up and maintain a database that will improve its gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. Also, they committed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Not Due to pandemic restrictions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:

a.	Designate new members of the Inventory Committee who will see to it that requirements relative to the conduct of complete physical count of all the property, plant and equipment of the Municipality at year-end are duly complied with to determine the validity, reliability and existence of its properties.  The committee shall reconcile the results of the count with the property and accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and

b.	require the ICO-Municipal Accountant to effect the prompt transfer of the cost of completed project from the Trust Fund to the General Fund and provide depreciation for all depreciable PPE as provided under PPSAS 17 to establish proper valuation of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: Some PPE accounts recorded in the Trust Fund books were already transferred to the General Fund
observations.reason: Inventory reports were still not prepared and submitted in CY 2020 and the ICO-Municipal Accountant still failed to provide allowance for depreciation for depreciable assets as accuracy of the stated values of PPEs in the books could not be verified due to the absence of turn-over documents from previous Treasurer and Supply Officer.

observations.managementAction: Soft copies of BRS for the months of January to September 2020 for Special Education Fund were furnished to the Audit Team on March 25, 2021.
observations.reason:
Bank Reconciliation Statements for General and Trust Fund account were not submitted as well as BRS under SEF for the months of October to December 2020.

Moreover, subsidiary ledgers for each depository account were still not submitted.

The ICO-Municipal Accountant explained that the delay in the preparation and submission of monthly bank reconciliation statement was mainly due to the delayed release of bank statements by the depository bank.

observations.managementAction:
Some procedural lapses noted in CY 2019 audit were already corrected by the BAC, such as:

Copy of invitations sent to at least three observers was already furnished to the Audit Team.
The invitation to bid was already posted   simultaneously in the PhilGEPS website and in the conspicuous places.
The Bid Evaluation Report was already prepared using the standard format (SF-INFRA-60).
The BAC had already furnished the Audit Team with the copy of notice issued to the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid.
observations.reason: Some lapses in procurement activities were still noted in CY 2020 audit as discussed in part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing, we recommended that charging of expenditures shall be limited to those with available approved appropriations only.  In cases where the appropriations are no longer sufficient to cover any expenditures, an appropriation ordinance shall first be passed and approved before payment of said expenditures be effected.  In the instant case, the concerned accountable officer shall be required to refund the cash advance granted without appropriations.

We also recommended and the management agreed that the ICO-Municipal Accountant be guided accordingly by prescribed accounting and auditing standards in recording all transactions in order to present fairly the financial statements.  Meantime, effect the corresponding adjustments for proper presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Disbursements made in CY 2020 were covered by approved appropriation ordinance. This is without reference to the lapses in the computation of savings declared as beginning balance in the annual budget.
observations.reason: Erroneous accounting entry made to record the subject disbursement was still not corrected in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor enjoin coordination between the Accounting, Treasurer and Budget Offices during the preparation of Annual Budget so as to ensure that the amount provided therein are accurately declared based on their existing records and reports.
observations.managementAction: Savings per budget items for CY 2019 as per computation prepared by the ICO-Municipal Accountant were properly presented.
observations.reason:
The deficiency noted in the computation of CY 2018 savings which was declared as beginning balance in CY 2019 annual budget was not considered in the computation of savings for CY 2019 which was declared as beginning balance in CY 2020 budget thus, resulting in a discrepancy of at least P8,634,845.63 between the computation of saving made by the ICO-Municipal Accountant and computation per audit.

The ICO-Municipal Accountant explained that the budget preparation for CY 2020 was already done prior to the receipt of AOM pertaining to the audit of the beginning available cash balance declared in prior year’s budget thus, it was not considered in the computation of CY 2019 savings which was declared as beginning balance in CY 2020.  Same observation was noted in CY 2020 audit.

observations.managementAction:
Financial assistance were immediately released to beneficiaries once the amount has been determined.

The amount of cash assistance to be given to the individuals in crisis situation was already indicated in the Certificate of Eligibility signed by the Municipal Mayor.
observations.reason:
Clear cut guidelines, policies and procedures on the selection and grant of livelihood assistance within the Municipality were still not established.

The Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant still failed to indicate the dates when they signed in the vouchers.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already stopped the operation in an open dump site as final garbage disposal facility.
observations.reason:
The Municipality failed to conduct regular monitoring of implementation of the plan and other relevant laws/ordinances within the Municipality’s jurisdiction.

Proper segregation of wastes at source was still not observed.

observations.managementAction:
GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2020 was reviewed by the DILG. A Certificate of Review and Endorsement dated June 24, 2020 was duly secured.

GAD Database was already established.

GAD Accomplishment report was submitted to the Audit Team.

GAD database maintained by the Municipality do not entirely conform with the data requirements for the LGU GAD Database.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing audit observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a.	Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to be guided by the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP.

Proper classification of PPAs in the LDRRMFIP should also be given careful attention such that PPAs which fall under the ambit of capital expenditures and are intended to be implemented in the ensuing years be properly classified as Capital Outlay;

b.	ICO-Municipal Accountant to (i) comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRM Fund set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; (ii) review the regularity and completeness of supporting documents of disbursement vouchers prescribed under Section 9.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012 - 001 before processing claims for payment; (iii) ensure that all disbursements are recorded/ accounted in the book of accounts to present reliable financial statements; (iv) submit the aforementioned lacking documents to the Audit Team to prove the transaction’s validity, accuracy, regularity and propriety; and (v) submit the notes comprising a summary of significant accounting policies and other explanatory notes; and

c.	BAC to strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 for the procurement activities of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for CY 2020 were submitted.
observations.reason:
Proper classification of PPAs in the LDRRMFIP was still not observed.

Accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRM Fund were still not observed by the ICO-Municipal Accountant.

Procedural lapses were still noted in the procurement of PPAs out of LDRRM Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to strictly adhere with the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in the preparation of Annual Development Plan to be funded out of the 20 percent DF to ensure that all items to be funded are development programs/projects and activities which are compliant with the guidelines. Also, refund the amount of disbursements which are not allowed in the 20 percent DF as enumerated in Item 4.0 of DILG and DBM JMC 2017-1 as written justification. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer give priority to the timely submission of the accounts and year-end financial reports within the prescribed period required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and Circular No. 2010-001 and Paragraph 2 of Section 41 of PD 1445 to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit thereof.                 We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor look into the possible cause of the delayed preparation and submission of the said reports and take immediate action to solve this problem.
observations.reason: Changes in administration/ leadership during CY 2019 led to changes in the designation of personnel in charge of the Accounting Department.  Since turn-over of accountabilities were not properly conducted, significant delays in the submission of monthly/ quarterly and annual accounts and financial reports were incurred.

Inventory reports were still not prepared and submitted in CY 2019.

Verification of CY 2019 records disclosed that no Journal Entry was made in the General Fund books to effect the transfer from Trust Fund to General Fund of Motor Vehicles and Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment amounting to P6,970,275.00 and P132,000.00, respectively, to transfer PPE accounts recorded in the Trust Fund books totaling P4,405,542.51 to the General Fund as well as to transfer to appropriate asset account the  completed projects recorded under Construction in Progress (CIP) account in the SEF and Trust Fund books amounting to P25,613,084.72 

Also, the accounting office still failed to provide depreciation for depreciable assets.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
the ICO-Municipal Treasurer maintain cashbook by fund and regularly update and reconcile records with the cash on hand daily or as necessary. Also, reconcile the cashbooks with the accounting records at least monthly to ensure that the recorded transactions are complete and accurate.
 the collecting officers observe daily remittance of collections and preparation and submission of corresponding RCDs to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to avoid delays in the reporting of cash collections and remittances;
the ICO-Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions. Adjust the balances of the accounts affected by the miscalculated collections and deposits.

We recommended that the concerned collecting officer submit written explanation relative to the above deficiencies noted. We also recommended that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer verify the remittances of collections of the accountable officers before acknowledging receipt of the submitted RCD to ensure that all collections are remitted properly and intact. Additionally, we recommended that the ICO-Municipal Accountant, being the internal auditor of the Municipality review the official receipts and deposit slips supporting the Report of Collections and Deposits submitted by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer prior to recording the same in the books to warrant that the report and its supporting documents are complete and valid.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate collecting officer who shall exclusively perform the collecting function pursuant to Section 50 of GAAM Volume III. The disbursing officer should not be allowed to perform collecting functions to avoid mishandling of government funds.
observations.reason:
Verification of transactions shows that the Municipal Treasurer still failed to deposit intact the collections for the CY 2019. No adjustment was being made by the Municipal Accountant with regard to the balances of the accounts affected by the miscalculated collections and deposits in CY 2018.

Accounting errors were still noted in the recording of collections and deposits for CY 2019.
Cash Advances totaling P7,745,924.67 remained unliquidated as at                     December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) facilitate establishment and maintenance of GAD database to have an effective basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation and to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are responsive to address the same.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to establish GAD database in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Office together with the Local Development Council judiciously and meticulously plan programs/projects/activities included in the Annual Investment Plan and promptly implement those priority projects in order to deliver satisfactory benefits to the constituents.
observations.reason:
Required detailed engineering activities have not been sufficiently carried out and adequate planning have not been done before the projects were proposed to be included in the list of the 20% priority projects.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to
complete the conduct of physical count of municipal properties; prepare the
RPCPPE and reconcile the same with the accounting records; and maintain Property
Cards for each PPE category to ascertain the existence and valuation of the recorded
properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• demand the immediate liquidation of the CAs from the concerned officials and
employees and cause the withholding of their salaries for failure to do so or
deduct the corresponding amounts from the money due them;
• refrain from granting additional cash advance to those with outstanding balance
and see to it that CAs are liquidated within the prescribed period; and
• prepare the supporting documents necessary for the filing of the request for
authority to write-off of dormant accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor file with the COA the
request for authority to write-off the dormant receivables together with the SB
Resolution and necessary supporting documents in compliance with COA Circular
No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
exert more effort in retrieving prior years’ records to identify the nature of the Land
Improvements, Aquaculture Structures and establish the correctness of the account
balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to
deposit all collections promptly and intact in compliance with the afore-cited
regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Administrator to
formulate strategies and implement measures to improve the financial performance of
the PUEE.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer to:
• submit to the Audit Team at the beginning of the year, the list of all ongoing
government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year and
the monthly monitoring report; and
• inform the Auditors within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project
or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project
signboards and/or public notices were already posted, for validation.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:

     a. Designate new members of the Inventory Committee who will see to  it
        that requirements relative to the conduct of complete physical count
        of all the property, plant and equipment of the Municipality at year-
        end are duly complied with to determine  the  validity,  reliability
        and existence of its properties.  The committee shall reconcile  the
        results of the count with the property and  accounting  records  and
        submit the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE to the  Audit  Team
        pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for  LGUs,  Volume  I;
        and

     b. require the ICO-Municipal Accountant to effect the  prompt  transfer
        of the cost of completed project from the Trust Fund to the  General
        Fund and provide depreciation for all depreciable  PPE  as  provided
        under PPSAS 17 to establish proper valuation of PPE accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation and the management agreed that  the
ICO-Municipal Accountant:

     a. strictly observe the  monthly  preparation  of  Bank  Reconciliation
        Statements to validate the balance of the Cash in  Bank  account  in
        the financial statements,  in  compliance  with  Section  3  of  COA
        Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996;

     b. exert diligent effort  in  identifying  the  individual  reconciling
        items presented in the BRS by preparing schedules and/or details  of
        the amount presented in lump sum;

     c. submit the bank statements and other relevant documents showing that
        the identified reconciling items were properly recorded and adjusted
        in the books of accounts; and

     d. maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for each depository account  to
        substantiate the  validity  and  correctness  of  balances  of  each
        account and to  easily  monitor  the  transactions  affecting  those
        accounts.

We  recommended  and  the  management  agreed  that  the   Municipal
Accountant prepare and submit the necessary schedule/aging of the  above-
mentioned  payable  accounts  as  required  under  Section  3.0  of   COA
Accounting Circular Letter No. 2007-002 for the Audit  Team  to  validate
the balances reported in the financial statements.

We recommended and the management agreed that  the  Municipal  Mayor
direct:

     a. the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Collecting Officers to  strictly
        observe the existing rules and regulations governing collections and
        deposits of government funds to avoid the possible loss  and  misuse
        of government funds;

     b.  the  ICO-Municipal  Treasurer  to  regularly  update  cashbook  and
        reconcile records with the cash  on  hand  daily  or  as  necessary.
        Also, reconcile the cashbooks with the accounting records  at  least
        monthly to ensure the completeness  and  accuracy  of  the  recorded
        transactions; and

     c. the ICO-Municipal Accountant review the  recorded  transactions  and
        make the necessary adjustments, where necessary.

annotated outline together with the comments and recommendations provided by
the NSWMC. He agreed and committed to comply with the audit recommendation.
Moreover, the MPDC commented that the Municipality is under the process of
converting the dumpsite into sanitary landfill.
observations.reason:
The SWMP is not yet approved by NSWMC and the closure of open dumpsite is not yet
completed.
See observation No.10,
Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the ICO – Municipal Accountant strictly observe the timely submission of the Financial Statements and supporting schedules and accounts (DVs/Payrolls/Official Receipts) to the Audit Team. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor look into the possible cause of the delayed preparation and submission of the said reports and take immediate action to solve this problem.
observations.managementAction: The OIC- Municipal Accountant once again failed to submit financial reports and its supporting schedules and accounts on the deadline set. Moreover, verification of the submitted reports revealed that said financial reports were incomplete, no supporting schedules attached and no General Ledger for all funds. It has come to the knowledge of the Audit Team that also the OIC Municipal Accountant just resigned from his post.
observations.reason: said financial reports were incomplete, no supporting schedules attached and no General Ledger for all funds. It has come to the knowledge of the Audit Team that also the OIC Municipal Accountant just resigned from his post.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MPDC and/or Municipal Engineer properly plan and monitor the implementation of development projects to maximize the use of 20% development fund for the benefit of the constituents. We recommended that the MPDO together with all other officials and employees concerned to facilitate adequate planning and budgeting for development projects. Identify the individual PPAs in the AIP and refrain from inputting lump sum/generic term appropriation. Submit the required document to prove the validity and legality of the projects implemented during the year. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials observe strictly Items 3.0 and 4.0 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM. Avoid charging expenses which do not constitute capital expenditures under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO and the Municipal Engineer commented that except for the Construction of the Multi-purpose Building, the rest of the programmed projects sourced from the 20% development fund were already implemented and completed as of December 2018 only that the contractors have not submitted their billings yet, hence, the fund remained unutilized as of year-end. As regards to the non-utilization of the fund intended for the Construction of Multi-purpose Building appropriated in CY 2017 and 2018, the Municipal Mayor explained that the said fund was not utilized for they are saving the fund for the construction of new Public Market which has an estimated contract cost of P35M. The management, however, informed the Audit Team that said project will be subjected for bidding after May 2019 election and promised to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The MPDO and the Municipal Engineer commented that except for the Construction of the Multi-purpose Building, the rest of the programmed projects sourced from the 20% development fund were already implemented and completed as of December 2018 only that the contractors have not submitted their billings yet, hence, the fund remained unutilized as of year-end. The Municipal Mayor explained that the said fund was not utilized for they are saving the fund for the construction of new Public Market which has an estimated contract cost of P35M.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 1. direct the submission of MSWMP to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for review and approval by the National Solid Waste Commission; 2. evaluate the current composition of the MSWMB and look for possible members that could qualify to complete the line up; 3. stop the operation on open dump site for garbage disposal, instead, develop a sanitary landfill as final garbage disposal site of the LGU; and 4. instruct the municipal officials concerned to review the budget set for Solid Waste Management for the effective and efficient performance of its programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality commented that their MSWMP is still pending for approval in the provincial level. As to operation for garbage disposal, they reasoned out that they have no options since the municipality has no budget to develop a sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: The Municipality commented that their MSWMP is still pending for approval in the provincial level. As to operation for garbage disposal, they reasoned out that they have no options since the municipality has no budget to develop a sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the LGU’s bank accounts to the Audit Team together with the paid checks, debit/credit memos for verification. Also make an extra effort to submit the bank reconciliation statements on time.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The newly hired OIC- Municipal Accountant has not yet submitted Bank Reconciliation Statements from January to December 2018 for all funds. According to the municipality, he resigned from his post and new OIC- Municipal Accountant from Budget Office was designated by the Municipal Mayor so as not to hamper the operation of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. the Municipal Accountant set up RPT/SET Receivables; b. the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a duly certified list of Real Property Taxpayers showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and c. the Municipal Assessor furnish the Treasurer with the latest valuations of property of each taxpayer.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: In CY 2018, the Municipal Accountant noted the observations and promised to implement in CY 2019. The exit conference on April 17, 2019: the newly designated OIC- Municipal Accountant noted the observations and promised to implement in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Retired Municipal Treasurer and the six (6) retired Collecting Officers turn over the Accountable Forms as presented in the attached Annexes G to L. We also recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials be vigilant so that time lapses in the reporting of collections and deposits be avoided to safeguard the Municipality’s resources.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The retired Municipal Treasurer and 6 collecting officers did not turn- over the balance of their accountability for AF. The Audit Team also noted that same lapses were incurred by new collectors under the supervision of OIC- Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the strict and uniform Municipal implementation of the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002; b. require all accountable officers and employees to settle/liquidate all their outstanding cash advances by sending confirmation, notification or demand letters; c. in case of failure to settle/liquidate their cash advances, cause or order the withholding of the payment of their salaries as provided for under Section 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and impose the sanctions provided under the penal provision of Section 128 of PD No. 1445 and/or study the possibility of filing the appropriate charges to enforce settlement thereof in accordance with Section 9.3.3 of the same Circular and Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code; and d. require an Office Clearance from Money and Property accountabilities, pursuant to Sections 200 and 256, Vol. I of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual from all personnel who seek retirement, resignation, transfer or claim for death benefits in order that all accountabilities could be appropriately settled.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal Mayor noted in CY 2018 that demand letters to all accountable officers and employees will be issued. Unliquidated cash advances accumulated to P7,189,421.75 as year- end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant carefully analyze and review the accounting entries made on the accounts affected, reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect appropriate adjustments thereof to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the agency’s financial position.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

Such case shall be charged immediately to the appropriate expense accounts. b. prepare JEVs to adjust/record issuances on the affected accounts and coordinate closely with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure reconciliation of their inventory records, also in respect of physical inventory taking. c. regularly prepare and submit accurate and complete Summary of Supplies and Materials Issue (SSMI) with supporting Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) pursuant to Section 121 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, to the Accounting Unit for proper recording/posting of issuance in the books of accounts and Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs).
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
